records.ety: [OF. despis-, in some forms of despire to despise, fr. L. despicere, despectum, to look down upon, despise; de- + spicere, specere, to look. See Spy, and cf. Despicable, Despite.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Despised (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Despising.]

defs: To look down upon with disfavor or contempt; to contemn; to scorn; to disdain; to have a low opinion or contemptuous dislike of.
quotes: Men naturally despise those who court them, but respect those who do not give way to them.
